Insights from Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, Time Magazine, The Washington Post, Nikkei Asia, and Reuters

Sign up for Semafor Flagship:The daily global news briefing you can trust.Read it now.

For a Chinese audience, the Paris Olympics opening ceremony Friday offered a rare glimpse of LGBTQ representation ГўВҖВ” and a chance to air grievances against state media censorship.

Broadcasters for CCTV appeared too stunned to speak during a drag performance and another involving men kissing. Afterward, the topics ГўВҖВңCCTV is silentГўВҖВқ and ГўВҖВңdrag queensГўВҖВқ trended on Chinese social media platform Weibo for a full day alongsidevideo clipsof the live broadcast from Paris.

Some users lamented missing the broadcast, which aired during the night in China, while others praised the Olympics for the display of diversity. ГўВҖВңIt was a beautiful night to stay up for,ГўВҖВқ wrote one Weibo user.



After fumbling the broadcast, Chinese social media users mocked CCTV as becoming ГўВҖВңWesternizedГўВҖВқ as a play on the governmentГўВҖВҷs stance that LGBTQ rights are aWestern ideathat threaten Chinese values. The phrase, however, has been used by Chinese people online to evade censorship and deride the authoritiesГўВҖВҷ draconian stance. The government has curbed college-run LGBTQ clubs, shut down social media accounts, and waged an online war against ГўВҖВңeffeminate men,ГўВҖВқ and essentially excluded LGBTQ people from public life. ГўВҖВңDespite morerestrictions on LGBT+ advocacy in China, the community has shown resilience,ГўВҖВқ Yuhsuan Chien, a China Dissent Monitor analyst, told Nikkei Aasia earlier this year, noting that government pressure has made online advocacy more common.

Across the strait in Taiwan, same-sex marriage is permitted (China does not allow it), as are Pride parades, LGBTQ films, and drag performances. Earlier this year, former president Tsai Ing-wen eveninvitedan award winning Taiwanese drag queen to perform at the Presidential Office Building. TaiwanГўВҖВҷs progress in LGBTQ rights in the generally conservative East Asia ГўВҖВ”ГӮВ it was the first Asian territory to legalize same-sex marriage in 2019 ГўВҖВ”ГӮВ has helped it forge a distinct national identity, anddistinguish itselffrom China, The Washington Post wrote. Support for the LGBTQ community has also become animportant aspectof young Taiwanese peopleГўВҖВҷs sense of national identity, Nikkei Asia reported.

title: Olympics offers China rare glimpse of LGBTQ representation | Semafor
description: The Paris GamesГўВҖВҷ opening ceremony also gave Chinese social media users a rare occasion to mock the countryГўВҖВҷs censorious state media.
